summary.intent: Fix two Codex review findings on PR #681 (ars-ui): controlled handoff in SyncProps and cancellation of stale day-period commit on CJK input resolution, then re-pass the Codex review.
summary.outcome: Both fixes were implemented with TDD (red→green), passed full verification (unit, spec conformance, format, clippy, coverage at 92.13%, proptest, mutation with 0 missed mutants), committed with co-author trailer, and pushed. A second Codex review pass was triggered.

repo: The service result layer exposes cancelled effects directly, enabling deterministic assertion of cancellation behavior without timing hacks.
cargo-mutants is run per-file on changed source (crates/ars-components/src/date_time/time_field/mod.rs) as part of the PR fix verification gate.
The project uses cargo xtask spec validate and a spec_corpus_compile_snippets test to verify spec conformance alongside unit tests.
Codex review state is tracked via GitHub issue reactions (👀 = reviewing, 👍 = done) polled with the GraphQL reviewThreads API.
CI checks run in this order: Snapshot Change Check → Formatting → Clippy → Workspace Check → Mutual Exclusion → Unit Tests.

code.finding: SyncProps handler was not marking prop-synced values as controlled; fixed to transition bindable into controlled mode on SyncProps(Some(value)).
CJK day-period disambiguation path was not cancelling the pending TypeBufferCommit effect; fixed to emit a cancel effect once the day-period input resolves.
Spec updated to document the cancellation rule for pending day-period commits on CJK disambiguation.

workflow: cargo test with two positional test name filters fails; each filter must be run as a separate cargo test invocation.
cargo-mutants runs a silent per-mutant phase after a noisy baseline phase; interrupting mid-run loses all progress.
The review loop is reaction-driven (👀/👍), not thread-count-driven; resolved threads alone do not signal Codex is done.
Heavy parallel checks (e.g. mutation + proptest simultaneously) are avoided to prevent distorting mutation timeout behavior.

friction: cargo test rejected two positional test name filters in a single invocation, requiring separate runs to confirm red state.
Codex review polling required many 2-minute sleep cycles (~30+ minutes total) before the second pass completed.
cargo-mutants ran silently for an extended period (10+ minutes) with no incremental output, making it hard to judge progress.
The mutation run processed 103 mutants sequentially, blocking the commit/push gate for the entire duration.
open_items: No missed mutants were found, but 6 mutants were marked unviable — worth reviewing whether those represent untestable code paths.
The second Codex review pass was still in progress (👀) at the end of the transcript; its final verdict is unknown.
CI Unit Tests were still running at the last observed poll; final CI green state not confirmed in transcript.
